commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=5b31f7ab6db7337e69c5f025c9bd87f9d415d8b7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes SSH-backed `crabbox status --wait` to call full lease resolution instead of status-only resolution and adds a CLI regression test for plain status versus wait behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, without executing the provider: current main uses `StatusOnly: true` for SSH-backed status, while Tenki status-only resolve returns no SSH target for readiness probing. The PR's timeout regression is also source-reproducible through Tenki full `Resolve` and `prepareLease`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up because the draft PR has a blocking timeout finding and missing real behavior proof that automation cannot provide for the contributor's Tenki setup.

target.title: fix(status): resolve ssh target while waiting
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep plain SSH-backed `crabbox status` on the read-only `StatusOnly` resolve path.
- Use full SSH resolve for `crabbox status --wait` so providers can return an SSH target for readiness probing.
- Add a regression test covering plain status vs wait resolve behavior.

This follows up the Tenki provider merge in https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/pull/231. Tenki status-only resolution is intentionally read-only, but the wait path needs SSH material to prove `ready=true`.

## Verification
- `go test ./internal/cli -run 'TestStatusWait|TestStatusWaitUsesFullSSHResolve'`
- `go test ./internal/providers/tenki ./internal/providers/all`
- `go vet ./...`
- `go test ./...`
target.author: aki-luxor
